action.content: Write a cover letter for a job in Ventura that is funny and would be enjoyed by someone named Darius, wrap the entire response in double quotation marks.
action.label: context

action.content:
"Dear Darius,

I am writing to apply for the position in Ventura, a city I believe is the only place on Earth where a job application can be as breezy and sun-drenched as the ocean views from the pier. I've heard you're looking for someone who can handle the unique rhythm of life in Ventura, and frankly, I think my resume is the perfect match for your vibe.

First, let's address the elephant in the room: the traffic. I promise I will not try to outrun a car on the 101. In fact, I've already started a GoFundMe to buy a unicycle just to get to work faster; if you don't hire me, I'll be the guy pedaling past your office while eating a burrito.

I bring to the table a unique blend of skills: I can surf the internet faster than a seagull stealing a hot dog, I know exactly which taco stand in Ventura has the best salsa (it's the one with the guy who wears a sombrero indoors, obviously), and I possess the rare ability to laugh at my own jokes, even the ones that fall flat like a deflated beach ball.

I am confident that my enthusiasm will bring a little more sunshine to your team, and I promise to treat your coffee breaks with the same reverence as a local festival. If you're ready to hire someone who can navigate the Ventura lifestyle without getting lost in the fog (or the traffic), I'd love to chat.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
P.S. If you don't hear back from me, please assume I've already moved to Santa Barbara to start a podcast about the merits of avocado toast."
action.label: response
